title: neoabbottia quadricostata
intro: Neoabbottia quadricostata, also known as the four-ribbed neoabbottia, is a flowering plant species within the family Gesneriaceae, native to the tropical regions of Sri Lanka and India. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, derived from its leaves and flowers. Consuming Neoabbottia quadricostata is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems. In traditional medicine, the plant is prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder for various herbal remedies, often used to treat colds, coughs, and other illnesses.
